'''Mark Tookey''' (born 9 March 1977) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990 and 2000s. He played as a {{rlp|pr}} in Australia for the [[South Queensland Crushers]] and the [[Parramatta Eels]] as well as the [[New Zealand Warriors]]. Tookey then played in the [[Super League]] for the [[Castleford Tigers]] and the [[Harlequins RL]].

==Background==
Tookey was born in [[Paddington, New South Wales]] and raised in [[Brisbane]], Queensland, playing his junior rugby league for [[Logan Brothers]] and Springwood Tigers.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newtownjets.com/flashback-friday-mark-tookey/|title=Flashback Friday – Mark Tookey|website=Newtown Jets}}</ref> In 1994, he represented the Queensland under-17 side.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://18thman.com/representative/under-17s-origin/1994-2/|title=Under 17 Origin 1994|website=18thman.com}}</ref>

==Playing career==
Tookey commenced his premiership playing career with the [[South Queensland Crushers]] debuting during the [[1996 ARL season]]. That season he represented the Queensland under-19 side.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://18thman.com/representative/under-19s-origin/1996-2/|title=Under 19 Origin 1996|website=18thman.com}}</ref> He then moved to the [[Parramatta Eels]] and later joined the [[Auckland Warriors]]. He played for the Warriors at prop forward in their [[2002 NRL Grand Final]] loss to the [[Sydney Roosters]].

In 2004 Tookey started playing in the [[Super League]] for English club the [[Castleford Tigers]], later moving to the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]]/[[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]].

==Post-playing==
Following to his retirement as a player, Tookey was in 2009 the assistant coach of the [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tongan side]].<ref>New Zealand Rugby League, ''The 2009 Kiwis'' (Auckland, Sentra Printing 2009) p.35</ref> He later worked as a Development Officer for the [[Canberra Raiders]] with their associated club [[Souths Logan Magpies]].<ref name="renegades">{{cite web|url=http://renegadesfs.com/index.php/nrl/nrl-news/item/387-meeting-mark-tookey|title=Meeting Mark Tookey|publisher=Renegades Fantasy Sports|date=17 September 2014|accessdate=26 January 2016}}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>

Tookey ran as an independent candidate in Division 5 in the 2016 [[Logan City]] Council election,<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/questnews/logan/former-nrl-hardman-mark-tookey-to-join-mal-meninga-and-glenn-lazarus-in-moving-into-politics/news-story/f41c7268691fab2acaa09dc43850c4f7|title=Former NRL hardman Mark Tookey to join Mal Meninga and Glenn Lazarus in moving into politics|newspaper=Courier Mail|date=6 August 2015|accessdate=26 January 2016}}</ref><ref name="Tookey 20160126">{{cite web|url=http://www.marktookey.com.au/index.php|title=Mark Tookey for Councillor|publisher=His own website|accessdate=26 January 2016}}{{Dead link|date=November 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref> which was being held on 19 March.

In November 2018, Tookey played for Parramatta in the Legends of League tournament held in Gosford. The side was captained by [[Nathan Hindmarsh]] and made it to the preliminary final before being defeated by The Barbarians.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2018/11/19/parramatta-at-legends-of-league-tournament/|title = Parramatta at Legends of League tournament|date = 18 November 2018}}</ref>

In November 2019, Tookey played for Parramatta in the Legends of League Tournament, held in Broadmeadow, NSW. The side was captained by [[Nathan Hindmarsh]], they made it to the grand final, and beating the Canterbury Team in the Final.
